title: Evidence Foundry Exact-Passage Assertion Document
description: Validates modules/<moduleId>/evidence-assertions.json (evidence-foundry-buildout P3-T3, FR-12 second half, `02 §4.10`, OQ-3/OQ-7). This is a new, module-package-scoped artifact type distinct from schemas/evidence.schema.json's source-level `evidence.json`: each record here binds one rf claim to one exact source passage (or, when rights are restricted, to that passage's immutable hash + precise selector only). Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, or that a named human clinician reviewed anything — `reviewBy`/`claimStatus` are traceability metadata, not sign-off. scripts/validate-kb.mjs wires this schema in as an EXISTENCE-GATED check: not every module carries this file (e.g. modules/anemia/ predates it), so its absence is not itself an error.

rfProvenance.description: The single upstream rf run/bundle every assertion in this file traces back to (mirrors modules/<id>/authoring-decisions.yaml's own `rfProvenance` block, P3-T1) — kept once at the document level rather than repeated per assertion.

assertions.description: `minItems: 0` (mirrors schemas/evidence.schema.json's own P1-T3 relaxation): a brand-new module package scaffold legitimately has zero assertions until Phase 3 projection populates it.

assertion.description: One exact-passage assertion (`02 §4.10`'s worked example), binding an `rf` claim to a specific source passage. `exactPassage` is an always-present key: the licensed short passage text when display is permitted, or the explicit JSON `null` when this plan's OQ-2 rights-restricted fallback applies (never an omitted key). `exactPassageSha256` is NOT unconditionally required at this top level — the allOf clause below is the sole authority requiring it, and requires it precisely when `exactPassage` is `null`, so a fixture record missing the hash in exactly that case (R-P2 analog) fails closed with one specific error, not a redundant pair.

rfRunId.description: Must equal the enclosing document's `rfProvenance.rfRunId` — this schema validates the assertion in isolation and cannot cross-check the parent; that equality is enforced by scripts/validate-kb.mjs.

rfSourceCardId.description: Preserve native rf source-card id verbatim (mirrors `02 §4.9`'s `rfSourceCardId` mapping rule).

sourceId.description: Compatibility alias matching schemas/evidence.schema.json's `$defs/source.id` pattern — the same alias modules/<id>/evidence.json's `sources[].id` uses for this source, once P3-T2 lands. This schema cannot cross-check that file; a cross-file join is a validate-kb.mjs / traceability-index concern (P5-T4), not this schema's.

rfEvidenceId.description: The rf source card's `extracted_points[].evidence_id` this assertion resolves to.

rfClaimId.description: The rf `claim_ledger.yaml` claim id this assertion grounds. For an inference-status claim (no `sources[]` of its own), this resolves instead to one of that claim's `inference_basis.from_claims[]` supported claims — an inference has no located passage to assert directly (`02 §4.11`).

passageId.description: `psg_<sha256>` (`02 §4.10`) — minted from the same digest as `exactPassageSha256` (with the `sha256:` prefix stripped), so the two fields are always in agreement by construction.

exactPassage.description: The licensed short passage text, or explicit `null` when OQ-2's rights-restricted fallback applies (this fixture: every one of `RF-CBC-001`'s 12 source cards carries `usage.allowed_for_public_output: false` with no passage positively confirmed rights-clear per tests/fixtures/rf-cbc-001/HASH-PROVENANCE.md, so every assertion projected from it is `null` here).

exactPassageSha256.description: Immutable SHA-256 digest of the original verbatim excerpt. REQUIRED (non-null) whenever `exactPassage` is `null` — enforced by the allOf conditional below, not merely by this type declaration (R-P2 analog: a record missing this field when the text is withheld must fail closed, not silently pass).

displayPolicy.description: `hash_and_selector_only` is the only legal value while `exactPassage` is `null` (enforced below). The other two values are schema-representable for a future positively-rights-cleared source; nobody asserts them today.

claimStatus.description: Copied from `claim_ledger.yaml.claims[].status` (`02 §4.11`'s routing table). Never mapped to a probability, confidence score, or patient-risk metric by this schema or any downstream consumer.

applicability.description: Population applicability of the passage. Both members independently nullable — `sex` is null whenever the source does not name a single sex (the common case), matching schemas/evidence.schema.json's existing `$defs/applicability` convention.

laboratory.description: Analyzer/assay context for the passage. Both members independently nullable: several passages in this bundle are outcome/registry findings with no analyzer-dependent numeric threshold at all (e.g. a referral-cohort proportion), and null here means exactly that, not an omission.

reviewBy.description: A governance housekeeping due-date for the next currency check on this assertion (mirrors `02 §4.9`'s `sources[].reviewBy` field). Not itself a clinical claim, threshold, or sign-off date.

allOf.description: OQ-2 rights-restricted fallback (`02 §4.10`): a null exactPassage must retain its immutable hash. Missing exactPassageSha256 here is exactly the seeded-invalid case this schema exists to reject (R-P2 analog).
A record whose text is withheld cannot simultaneously claim an excerpt-bearing display policy.

locator.description: Mirrors schemas/evidence.schema.json's `$defs/sourceLocator` shape (raw always present; the four parsed members independently nullable) plus the `02 §4.10` worked example's page/section/table/paragraph field names.
